title: Terpsichore subtilis
taxonomy: Terpsichore subtilis belongs to the family Polypodiaceae, a family of ferns commonly known as the 'polypody' family. This species is further classified as a member of the genus Terpsichore, which is a group of ferns native to the Americas. The family Polypodiaceae is itself part of the larger order Polypodiales, which comprises various families of ferns. The order Polypodiales is a subgroup of the class Polypodiopsida, which includes all types of ferns. This classification helps to understand the evolutionary relationships and characteristics of Terpsichore subtilis and other related species.

intro: Terpsichore subtilis, also known as Copaiba, is a plant species that belongs to the Copaifera genus and is native to the Amazon rainforest. The health benefits of Copaiba include its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which have been traditionally used to treat skin conditions, wounds, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Copaiba include sesquiterpenes, particularly beta-caryophyllene, which is responsible for its therapeutic effects.
